messageFlow.summary: WhatsApp message → trigger check (DMs always, groups @mention/prefix only) → cortex parallel fan-out (classification + memory + identity + dreams + insights + lquorum + speculative web prefetch all fire concurrently via Promise.all) → route to MiniMax or Claude → tool execution loop (up to 5 iterations, web_search checks prefetch cache first) or task planner (multi-step) → response sent. The cortex layer replaced the old sequential pipeline — all intelligence streams now race in parallel, total gather time equals the slowest stream (~1s) instead of the sum of all streams (~3s). Two addressing modes: 'clawd' for full advisory mode (planner available), 'clawdsec' for secretary/admin mode (single-tool fast path).

router.layer1: 4B classifier (PRIMARY) — Qwen3-4B on EVO port 8085. Every message goes through this. Returns category + needsPlan + confidence. ~200ms. Handles all routing decisions including whether a request needs multi-step planning.
router.layer2: Keyword heuristics (FALLBACK) — only fires when 4B is unavailable (EVO down, circuit breaker open). Category-specific regex patterns. Cannot detect needsPlan.
router.layer3: 0.6B LLM classifier (LAST RESORT FALLBACK) — Qwen3-0.6B on EVO port 8081. Only fires if both the 4B classifier AND keyword heuristics fail or are unavailable. Not used for engagement decisions — groups are @mention-only.
router.layer4: Default — PLANNING with Claude/MiniMax, guaranteed correct but costs money.
router.writeDetection: Any mutation intent (email drafts, calendar creates, soul changes) forces Claude — hallucinated args from local models cause real damage.
router.learnedRules: Self-improvement cycle adds new keyword rules overnight (data/learned-rules.json), loaded every 5 minutes, validated against eval suite.
router.secretaryMode: 'clawdsec' prefix bypasses the task planner entirely — needsPlan forced false regardless of what the 4B classifier thinks. Fast path for simple admin: check calendar, add todo, search email.

modelSelection.minimax_default: MiniMax M2.7 — default cloud model for ALL chat responses. ~$0.003-0.01 per call. Anthropic-compatible API.
modelSelection.claude_premium: Claude Opus 4.6 — premium model, only on explicit request ('ask claude', 'use opus'). Also quality gate for complex categories. ~$0.01-0.05 per call.
modelSelection.local_support: EVO models handle classification (4B primary, 0.6B last-resort fallback), vision (VL-30B), document summarisation, and embeddings. Never generate chat responses.
modelSelection.classifier_4b: Qwen3-4B on EVO (port 8085) — primary router. Category + needsPlan for every message. ~200ms.
